summary: The post explains why Wes McKinney and his team chose to join Cloudera after building DataPad, a next‑generation BI and visual analytics effort. He argues that traditional BI, centered on SQL-on-a-database, no longer fits a world of fragmented, cloud-hosted data and heavy ETL burdens. DataPad pursued a more agile, iterative architecture with substantial new backend infrastructure and high‑performance interactivity. As the company faced a major inflection point, he concluded that the biggest blocker for next‑gen BI is systems and data management rather than UI or collaboration. Joining Cloudera is framed as a way to amplify impact by improving the underlying data infrastructure and developer tooling for the broader ecosystem.
key_points: Legacy BI architectures built around SQL queries to a single data store are increasingly inadequate for modern, siloed data realities.
DataPad intentionally built a new architecture and backend system to enable faster, more iterative analytics at scale.
The team believed the critical bottleneck for next‑generation BI is data management and systems engineering, not just front‑end design.
Cloudera’s leadership in high‑performance analytics and shared system challenges made it the best place to maximize impact.
Improving developer tooling and data‑science productivity remains a central goal, aligned with Cloudera’s open source investments.
money_quotes: We saw a clear need for a next-generation business intelligence / visual analytics product.
The amount of planning and ETL work needed to make an existing BI system "fit" is frequently prohibitive.
We made an early decision to design a new architecture to enable the BI process to be more agile and iterative.
The system we built, code-named Badger, delivered an interactive analytics experience that exceeded our own expectations in performance and truly delighted our users.
It was clear to us that what's holding back next-generation BI/visual analytics is much more on the data management and systems side.
So the question came down to: continue building a standalone data product and duke it out in the marketplace, or use our systems expertise to accelerate the rising tide that will lift all ships.
On this front, we're really looking forward to working together; there is a lot of work to be done to enable developers and data scientists to get value out of their data faster and more intuitively.
key_insight: The real leverage for next‑generation analytics lies in advancing data systems and infrastructure, so joining a platform leader like Cloudera can have broader ecosystem impact than building a standalone BI product.
